explanation: The LTL property states that globally, whenever the system is operating in 'PSV mode', two conditions must concurrently hold. First, 'min_exp_time_psv' must be equal to half of the last inspiration time, which is directly represented by the atomic proposition 'minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2'. Second, 'min_exp_time_psv' must fall within the specified interval [0.4, 2] seconds. This interval constraint is captured by the conjunction of 'minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4' (min_exp_time_psv is greater than or equal to 0.4 seconds) and 'minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2' (min_exp_time_psv is less than or equal to 2 seconds). The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures that this implication holds true at all times.

The natural language statement specifies two conditions that must hold when the system is in 'PSV mode'. The first condition is that 'min_exp_time_psv' must be half of the last inspiration time, which is directly captured by the atomic proposition "minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2". The second condition is that 'min_exp_time_psv' must be within the interval [0.4 : 2] seconds. This is captured by the conjunction of "minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4" and "minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2". Since these conditions must always hold whenever the system is in PSV mode, a global implication is used: 'G (PSVMode -> (condition1 & condition2))'.

The natural language statement specifies conditions that must hold when the system is in 'PSV mode'. The 'shall be' implies a global invariant. The first condition, 'min_exp_time_psv shall be the half of the last inspiration time', is directly captured by the atomic proposition 'minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2'. The second condition, 'min_exp_time_psv shall be in the interval [0.4 : 2] sec.', is a conjunction of two conditions: 'min_exp_time_psv greater than or equal to 0.4 sec' and 'min_exp_time_psv less than or equal to 2 sec'. These are directly captured by the atomic propositions 'minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4' and 'minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2' respectively. Therefore, the LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV mode, then all three conditions must hold simultaneously.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV mode, then three conditions must simultaneously hold: 'min_exp_time_psv' must be equal to half of the last inspiration time, 'min_exp_time_psv' must be greater than or equal to 0.4 seconds, and 'min_exp_time_psv' must be less than or equal to 2 seconds. This directly translates the natural language requirement that in PSV mode, 'min_exp_time_psv' is both half of the last inspiration time and falls within the specified interval.

The natural language statement specifies conditions that must hold when the system is in 'PSV mode'. The 'shall be' implies that these conditions are universally true whenever 'PSV mode' is active. Therefore, a global (G) operator is used. The conditions are combined using logical AND (&): first, 'min_exp_time_psv shall be the half of the last inspiration time' is translated to 'minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2'; second, 'min_exp_time_psv shall be in the interval [0.4 : 2] sec.' is broken down into two parts, 'min_exp_time_psv is greater than or equal to 0.4 seconds' and 'min_exp_time_psv is less than or equal to 2 seconds', which are translated to 'minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4' and 'minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2' respectively. All these conditions are universally true whenever PSVMode is active.

The natural language statement specifies conditions that must hold true when the system is in 'PSV mode'. The 'G' (globally) operator ensures that the subsequent implication is always true. The implication 'PSVMode -> (...)' means that if the system is in PSV mode, then the conditions specified for 'min_exp_time_psv' must hold. These conditions are: first, 'min_exp_time_psv' must be equal to half of the last inspiration time, captured by 'minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2'. Second, 'min_exp_time_psv' must be within the interval [0.4 : 2] seconds, which is expressed by the conjunction of 'minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4' and 'minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2'. All three conditions must be true simultaneously when in PSV mode, hence they are combined with the '&' (AND) operator.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode (PSVMode), then three conditions must simultaneously hold: min_exp_time_psv must be equal to half of the last inspiration time (minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2), min_exp_time_psv must be greater than or equal to 0.4 seconds (minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4), and min_exp_time_psv must be less than or equal to 2 seconds (minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2). This captures the requirement that these conditions 'shall be' true when in PSV mode, and the interval constraint is represented by the conjunction of the lower and upper bounds.

The natural language statement specifies conditions that must hold when the system is in 'PSV mode'. The first condition is that 'min_exp_time_psv' (minimum expiratory time in PSV) must be half of the last inspiration time. This is directly represented by the atomic proposition 'minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2'. The second part of the statement specifies that 'min_exp_time_psv' must be within the interval [0.4, 2] seconds. This translates into two separate conditions: 'min_exp_time_psv' must be greater than or equal to 0.4 seconds, represented by 'minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4', and 'min_exp_time_psv' must be less than or equal to 2 seconds, represented by 'minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2'. All these conditions must hold whenever the system is in 'PSVMode'. Therefore, a global implication is used, stating that globally, if 'PSVMode' is true, then all three specified conditions must also be true.

The natural language statement specifies conditions that must hold true when the system is in 'PSV mode'. The 'shall be' implies that these conditions are invariants, meaning they must hold globally whenever the system is in PSV mode. The first part, 'min_exp_time_psv shall be the half of the last inspiration time', is directly captured by the atomic proposition 'minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2'. The second part, 'min_exp_time_psv shall be in the interval [0.4 : 2] sec.', is broken down into two conjuncts: 'min_exp_time_psv is greater than or equal to 0.4 sec' and 'min_exp_time_psv is less than or equal to 2 sec'. These are represented by 'minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4' and 'minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2' respectively. All these conditions must hold concurrently. Therefore, the LTL property states that Globally (G), if 'PSVMode' is active, then it must always be the case that 'minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2' AND 'minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4' AND 'minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2' are true.

The natural language statement specifies properties that must hold true when the system is in 'PSV mode'. The 'shall be' phrasing indicates that these are global invariants. Therefore, the LTL formula uses the 'G' (Globally) operator. The condition 'PSV mode' acts as a precondition, so an implication (->) is used: 'G (PSVMode -> ...)' meaning 'Globally, if PSVMode is active, then the following conditions must hold'. The conditions related to 'min_exp_time_psv' are:
  1. 'min_exp_time_psv shall be the half of the last inspiration time': This directly translates to the atomic proposition 'minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2'.
  2. 'min_exp_time_psv shall be in the interval [0.4 : 2] sec.': This is a conjunction of two conditions: 'min_exp_time_psv is greater than or equal to 0.4 seconds' and 'min_exp_time_psv is less than or equal to 2 seconds'. These translate to 'minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4' and 'minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2' respectively.
All these conditions within the PSV mode must hold concurrently, hence they are combined using the logical 'AND' (&) operator.
